name: Jerked Pork and Mushroom Stack
recipeIngredient: 1 lb pork tenderloin, trimmed of excess fat
4 Portobello mushrooms, stems removed and cleaned
2 tablespoons Paul Prudhomme's Pork and Veal Magic
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 250 degrees.
2. Slice the pork into 4 equal pieces and pound between sheets of plastic wrap until thin.
3. Rub mushrooms with roasted garlic, some olive oil, and season with Vegetable Magic and Pork and Veal Magic.
4. Rub pork with wet jerk seasoning and sprinkle with 3 tablespoons dry jerk seasoning. Dip in remaining olive oil.
5. Heat a large sauté pan until almost smoking. Sear pork slices for about 40 seconds per side. Remove to an oven-safe dish.
6. Add mushrooms to the pan and sauté for 1 minute per side, or until softened. Place mushrooms with pork and keep warm in oven.
7. In the same pan, pour cream and add coriander, allspice, remaining dry jerk seasoning, and Worcestershire sauce. Reduce until thickened, then stir in butter. Season with salt if needed.
8. Serve 1/4 of rice on plates, place pork slice on top, garnish with mushroom slices, and spoon sauce over the dish. Scatter green onions for garnish.
Yield: 4 servings
